interface.name: Bun
interface.summary: fast all-in-one JavaScript runtime, bundler, test runner and package manager
interface.description: Bun is an all-in-one toolkit for JavaScript and TypeScript apps. It ships as a single executable called bun and includes a fast JavaScript runtime (a drop-in replacement for Node.js), a bundler, a test runner, and a Node.js-compatible package manager.

interface.name: Electron
interface.summary: framework for creating native applications with web technologies
interface.description: Electron is a framework for creating native applications with web technologies like JavaScript, HTML, and CSS.

interface.name: NW.js
interface.summary: write desktop applications with all Web technologies
interface.description: NW.js (previously known as node-webkit) lets you call all Node.js modules directly from DOM and enables a new way of writing applications with all Web technologies.

interface.name: NW.js SDK
interface.description: This is the full NW.js plus additional DevTools for debugging.

interface.name: Node.js
interface.description: Node.js is a JavaScript runtime built on Chrome's V8 JavaScript engine. Node.js uses an event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and efficient.

entry-point.name: Corepack
entry-point.summary: Zero-runtime-dependency package acting as bridge between Node projects and their package managers

interface.name: pnpm
interface.summary: fast, disk space efficient package manager for JavaScript
interface.description: pnpm is a fast, disk space efficient package manager for JavaScript. It uses a content-addressable filesystem to store all files from all module directories on a disk, avoiding duplication and speeding up installation.
